content:
I have successfully completed all 3 CBSE Class 12 English Core Prediction Papers for the 2027 board exam. Here's the summary:

## Files Created

1. **D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\English Core\Prediction Papers\English_Core_Prediction_Paper_1.md** (25.6 KB)
2. **D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\English Core\Prediction Papers\English_Core_Prediction_Paper_2.md** (26.0 KB)
3. **D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\English Core\Prediction Papers\English_Core_Prediction_Paper_3.md** (28.8 KB)

## Analysis of CBSE Format (from 2024, 2025-26 SQP, and 2026 papers)

I successfully extracted and analysed the following papers:
- **2026 Board Paper** (1-3-1_Eng_lish Core.pdf) — full text extraction
- **2025-26 SQP** (EnglishCore-SQP.pdf) — full text extraction  
- **2024 Board Paper** (1_1_1_English Core.pdf) — partial text extraction
- *(2025 paper was scanned/image-based, no text extractable)*

### Confirmed Format Structure (Total: 80 Marks, 13 Questions)

| Section | Marks | Questions |
|---------|-------|-----------|
| **Section A – Reading Skills** | 22 | Q1 (12m) – Prose passage with 10 sub-questions; Q2 (10m) – Case study/data-based passage with 8 sub-questions |
| **Section B – Creative Writing Skills** | 18 | Q3 (4m) – Notice; Q4 (4m) – Invitation/Reply; Q5 (5m) – Letter/Job Application; Q6 (5m) – Article/Report |
| **Section C – Literature** | 40 | Q7 (6m) – Poetry extract (6×1); Q8 (4m) – Vistas extract (4×1); Q9 (6m) – Flamingo prose extract (6×1); Q10 (10m) – 5/6 short answers Flamingo (5×2); Q11 (4m) – 2/3 short answers Vistas (2×2); Q12 (5m) – Long answer Flamingo; Q13 (5m) – Long answer Vistas |

## Paper Differentiation

### Paper 1 Focus:
- **Reading:** Libraries in the 21st century; Mental health among students (case study with table)
- **Poetry:** An Elementary School Classroom in a Slum / A Thing of Beauty
- **Vistas Extract:** The Enemy / Memories of Childhood
- **Prose Extract:** Lost Spring / Poets and Pancakes
- **Long Answers:** Last Lesson + Indigo; Rattrap + Going Places; On the Face of It; The Third Level

### Paper 2 Focus:
- **Reading:** Handwriting in the digital age; Water scarcity across Indian states (case study with table)
- **Poetry:** Keeping Quiet / Aunt Jennifer's Tigers
- **Vistas Extract:** On the Face of It / The Tiger King
- **Prose Extract:** The Rattrap / Going Places
- **Long Answers:** Lost Spring + A Roadside Stand; Poets and Pancakes + Going Places; The Enemy; The Tiger King

### Paper 3 Focus:
- **Reading:** Art of storytelling; Online vs traditional education (case study with table)
- **Poetry:** My Mother at Sixty-six / A Roadside Stand
- **Vistas Extract:** The Enemy / The Third Level
- **Prose Extract:** Indigo / Journey to the End of the Earth
- **Long Answers:** Deep Water + Aunt Jennifer's Tigers; Last Lesson + Indigo; Journey to the End of the Earth; On the Face of It

### Key Features of All Papers:
- ✅ Exact CBSE format with standard header and general instructions
- ✅ Complete realistic reading passages (400+ words each)
- ✅ Case study passages with data tables
- ✅ All question types: MCQs, fill-in-the-blanks, assertion-reason, analogies, short answer, long answer
- ✅ Internal choice (OR) in every question that requires it
- ✅ All prescribed Flamingo and Vistas chapters covered across the 3 papers
- ✅ All 6 poetry texts covered across the 3 papers
- ✅ Appropriate word limits mentioned (40-50 words, 120-150 words)
- ✅ Varied writing tasks: notices, invitations, replies, letters to editor, job applications, articles, reports
